title: CARETAKER-WORKFLOW-DEEP
scope: Deepen the caretaker workflow now that Phase 79 made the caretaker the water inputter and the hubs reachable. Scout findings: caretaker = User role=caretaker + landlord_id, assigned to buildings via buildings.caretaker_id (+ CaretakerAssignment audit pending/accepted/declined); tickets auto-assign to building.caretaker_id on creation (TicketObserver). The caretaker dashboard (DashboardService::getCaretakerDashboardData) returns actionItems + todaysTasks (top 10 open, priority-sorted) + ticketStats + unitStats + hasWaterEnabled + landlord contact, rendered in Caretaker/Dashboard.vue (responsive but not a mobile-first actionable board; tasks only LINK to tickets.show — no inline actions). Tickets have full status lifecycle (open/acknowledged/in_progress/resolved/closed/cancelled) + SLA columns (sla_due_at/first_response_at/resolution_due_at/resolved_at); caretaker can update/resolve/comment. THREE GAPS: (1) the daily-task UX is link-only, not a mobile-first board with inline status actions; (2) ESCALATION DOES NOT EXIST — no escalate action, no escalated_* columns, no event/listener, no landlord view of stuck tickets (the vendor decline→notify-landlord flow is the closest pattern); (3) NO caretaker performance analytics — VendorPerformanceService (Phase 75: within-SLA%/avg-resolution/open-overdue/cost-per-ticket, batched grouped queries keyed by vendor_id, landlord-scoped) is the EXACT template for a CaretakerPerformanceService keyed by tickets.assigned_to + water_readings.recorded_by. Goals: mobile-first daily task board with inline actions + caretaker→landlord escalation routing + landlord-side caretaker performance dashboard. Reuse: VendorPerformanceService/MaintenanceVendorPerformanceController/VendorPerformance.vue pattern; the vendor accept/decline event+listener+activity-log pattern; TicketActivity first_response_at stamping; WithLandlordScope; HubShell/nav. All landlord-scoped. selectRaw static aggregates are the accepted pattern (no user-input interpolation).

findings.title: Mobile-first caretaker daily task board
findings.evidence: The caretaker's actionable surface is the dashboard todaysTasks list (DashboardService:379) which only links to tickets.show — no inline actions, not optimized as a mobile-first 'what do I do today' board. Caretakers work on phones in the field.
findings.remediation: NEW CaretakerTaskController::index (role:caretaker) + route tasks.index (/my-tasks) rendering Caretaker/TaskBoard.vue — a mobile-first board of the caretaker's OPEN assigned tickets grouped by urgency (overdue → urgent → today), each card showing building/unit, priority, age, and overdue (resolution_due_at < now) badge. Large tap targets, single-column on mobile. Link it from the caretaker dashboard + nav. Phase80 test: caretaker sees only their assigned open tickets; another caretaker's tickets excluded.

findings.title: Inline status actions from the board (acknowledge / start / resolve)
findings.evidence: A caretaker must open each ticket to change status. On mobile in the field, inline one-tap transitions are the whole point of a task board.
findings.remediation: CaretakerTaskController::transition (POST /my-tasks/{ticket}/transition) — guarded assigned_to === caretaker (NOT just landlord-scoped) + valid forward transition (open→acknowledged→in_progress→resolved); reuse Ticket status + TicketActivity logging (so first_response_at stamps). Resolve accepts optional notes. Board cards carry Acknowledge/Start/Resolve buttons that POST + optimistically update. Phase80 test: forward transition works for the assignee; a non-assignee caretaker is 403; backward/invalid transition rejected.

findings.title: Board surfaces water-due + escalation state
findings.evidence: The board should be the caretaker's single daily view: it must also surface whether water readings are due (when the water module is on) and which tickets they've already escalated (so they don't re-escalate).
findings.remediation: TaskBoard includes a water-record CTA when WaterModuleAccess::enabledForLandlord (links water.hub record tab) and marks tickets the caretaker has an OPEN escalation on (escalated_at set, not yet acknowledged) with an 'Escalated' chip. Phase80 test: water CTA hidden when module disabled; escalated ticket shows the chip.

findings.title: All-caught-up empty state + quick links
findings.evidence: An empty board should reassure, not look broken.
findings.remediation: Empty state ('All caught up') + quick links (record water if enabled, report issue, view all tickets). i18n keys en/sw/ar. Phase80 surface assertion.

findings.title: Escalation schema + Ticket scope + activity types
findings.evidence: No escalation exists. A caretaker who is stuck has only an internal comment the landlord must happen to notice. tickets has no escalated_* columns; TicketActivity has no escalated action.
findings.remediation: Migration: tickets.escalated_at (nullable ts), escalated_by (nullable FK users nullOnDelete), escalation_reason (nullable text), escalation_acknowledged_at (nullable ts), escalation_acknowledged_by (nullable FK users). Ticket::scopeEscalated (escalated_at NOT NULL AND escalation_acknowledged_at NULL) + isEscalated() + casts. TicketActivity ACTION_ESCALATED + ACTION_ESCALATION_ACKNOWLEDGED constants. Phase80 test: scopeEscalated filters open escalations only.

findings.title: TicketEscalationService + caretaker escalate action + event/listener
findings.evidence: No service/endpoint to escalate; no landlord notification (the vendor-decline flow is the model: clear state + log + event + notify landlord).
findings.remediation: App\Services\Maintenance\TicketEscalationService::escalate(Ticket, User caretaker, string reason) in a transaction: guard assigned_to===caretaker + not resolved/closed/cancelled + no existing OPEN escalation (idempotent); set escalated_at/by/reason; log TicketActivity(ACTION_ESCALATED); dispatch TicketEscalated. NotifyLandlordOnTicketEscalation listener → NotificationService in-app to the landlord (+ mailable, queued). EscalateTicketRequest authorize (assigned_to===caretaker). POST /my-tasks/{ticket}/escalate (role:caretaker). Phase80 test: escalate sets columns + logs + notifies landlord; non-assignee 403; double-escalate is a no-op/blocked.

findings.title: Escalation reason presets + free text + length guard
findings.evidence: Escalation needs a structured reason so the landlord can triage (parts needed, access denied, beyond scope, vendor needed, other).
findings.remediation: config maintenance.escalation_reasons presets; EscalateTicketRequest validates reason required|string|max:1000 (+ optional preset key). Board escalate modal offers presets + free text. Phase80 test: missing reason 422; over-long reason rejected.

findings.title: SLA-breach auto-escalation hook (opt-in)
findings.evidence: Today TicketSlaBreached only notifies; a resolution-SLA breach on a caretaker-assigned ticket is exactly 'stuck' and should optionally auto-escalate so the landlord has a single escalation queue.
findings.remediation: Opt-in listener on TicketSlaBreached(type=resolution) → if assigned to a caretaker and not already escalated, TicketEscalationService::escalate(system, reason='sla_breach') gated by config maintenance.auto_escalate_on_sla_breach (default false). Idempotent (no duplicate). Phase80 test: with flag on, a resolution breach escalates once; flag off, no escalation.

findings.title: Escalation i18n + activity-feed rendering
findings.evidence: Escalation strings + the new activity types need en/sw/ar + to render in the ticket activity feed.
findings.remediation: lang maintenance.escalation.* (en/sw/ar curated) + the ticket activity feed renders ACTION_ESCALATED / ACTION_ESCALATION_ACKNOWLEDGED with reason + actor. Phase80 surface assertion + lang parity.

findings.title: Landlord escalation queue + acknowledge/take-over
findings.evidence: The landlord has no way to see escalated tickets or act on them — escalation is useless without a landlord queue.
findings.remediation: Tickets index (landlord) gains an 'Escalated' filter/scope (Ticket::scopeEscalated) + an escalated badge/count. TicketEscalationService::acknowledge(Ticket, User landlord) sets escalation_acknowledged_at/by + logs ACTION_ESCALATION_ACKNOWLEDGED (resolving the open escalation); reassigning to another caretaker (existing tickets.assign) also clears the open escalation. POST /tickets/{ticket}/escalation/acknowledge (role:landlord). Phase80 test: landlord lists escalated; acknowledge clears it; reassign clears it.

findings.title: Escalation badge on landlord dashboard + nav
findings.evidence: The landlord should notice escalations without hunting the tickets list.
findings.remediation: Landlord dashboard actionItem 'Escalated tickets' (count of open escalations, links to the escalated filter) + a nav badge (navBadges) on Maintenance. Count is landlord-scoped + batched. Phase80 test: count reflects open escalations only (acknowledged excluded).

findings.title: Escalation visible on the ticket detail
findings.evidence: The ticket Show page should show the open-escalation banner (reason + who + when) with an acknowledge action for the landlord.
findings.remediation: Tickets/Show renders an escalation banner when isEscalated() with reason/actor/age + landlord acknowledge button. Phase80 surface assertion.

findings.title: CaretakerPerformanceService (mirror VendorPerformanceService)
findings.evidence: No caretaker analytics exist; the landlord can't see who is responsive/effective. All signals exist (tickets.assigned_to + first_response_at + resolved_at + resolution_due_at; water_readings.recorded_by).
findings.remediation: App\Services\Maintenance\CaretakerPerformanceService::forLandlord(int landlordId, int windowDays=90): batched grouped queries keyed by assigned_to over the landlord's caretakers → resolved_count, within_sla / within_sla_pct (resolved_at <= resolution_due_at), avg_resolution_hours, avg_first_response_hours (first_response_at - created_at), open_count, open_overdue (breachedResolutionSla), water_readings_recorded (recorded_by, approved, in window), escalations_raised (count). Landlord-scoped; no N+1; selectRaw static aggregates. Phase80 test: metrics correct for a caretaker with mixed resolved/overdue tickets.

findings.title: Landlord caretaker-performance page + route + nav
findings.evidence: The service needs a surface; mirror MaintenanceVendorPerformanceController + VendorPerformance.vue.
findings.remediation: MaintenanceCaretakerPerformanceController::index (role:landlord, WithLandlordScope, window 30/90/365) → Maintenance/CaretakerPerformance.vue (sortable table, within_sla_pct default desc, nulls as —, low-SLA highlighted) + route maintenance.caretaker-performance + a link from the Maintenance hub (and reachable per the Phase-79 nav-audit). Phase80 test: route renders with caretaker rows; nav-audit stays clean.

findings.title: Weekly caretaker performance rollup gauge
findings.evidence: Ops needs visibility on caretaker within-SLA over time (mirrors maintenance:cost-rollup + property:benchmark-rollup).
findings.remediation: caretaker:performance-rollup command (weekly Sun 05:10 Africa/Nairobi, in the Sunday rollup cluster, onOneServer) emitting landlord_caretaker_within_sla_pct{landlord_id,caretaker_id} gauge — visibility-only, no alert. Phase80 test: command exits 0.

findings.title: Performance page i18n + empty state
findings.evidence: Strings + a no-caretakers empty state need en/sw/ar.
findings.remediation: lang caretaker_perf.* (en/sw/ar curated) + empty state when the landlord has no caretakers. Phase80 surface assertion + lang parity.

findings.title: Phase80CaretakerWorkflowSurfaceTest consolidated watchdog
findings.evidence: Five categories need a single presence/behaviour map.
findings.remediation: Phase80CaretakerWorkflowSurfaceTest: schema (escalated_* columns), services bound (TicketEscalationService, CaretakerPerformanceService), routes registered (tasks.index, tasks.transition, tasks.escalate, tickets.escalation.acknowledge, maintenance.caretaker-performance), rollup command exits 0, nav-audit clean (new pages linked), lang parity (maintenance.escalation.*, caretaker_perf.*).

findings.title: docs/runbooks/caretaker.md + closeout + memory
findings.evidence: No caretaker runbook; the escalation flow + performance metrics + task board need an operator reference.
findings.remediation: docs/runbooks/caretaker.md (NEW): task board, escalation lifecycle (escalate → notify → acknowledge/reassign → auto-escalate flag), performance metric definitions + rollup. scripts/phase80-closeout.mjs + memory updates (phase80 plan, forward-path, pending-path, MEMORY.md).

closeout.summary: Deepened the caretaker workflow. ESCALATION: escalated_* columns + Ticket::scopeEscalated/isEscalated + TicketActivity ACTION_ESCALATED/ACKNOWLEDGED; TicketEscalationService::escalate (assignee-guard, terminal-guard, idempotent on open escalation, locks row, logs, dispatches TicketEscalated) + acknowledge; EscalateTicketRequest (assignee-only) + reason presets (config maintenance.escalation_reasons) + free text; NotifyLandlordOnTicketEscalation (in-app, auto-discovered); opt-in AutoEscalateOnSlaBreach on TicketSlaBreached(resolution) (config flag, idempotent). TASK-BOARD: CaretakerTaskController index/transition/escalate + tasks.* routes (role:caretaker) + Caretaker/TaskBoard.vue (mobile-first, grouped overdue/urgent/today, inline forward-only assignee-only status actions, water CTA, escalated chip, empty state) + caretaker nav entry. ESCALATION-VIEW: tickets index ?escalated filter (scopeEscalated) + landlord dashboard Escalated-Tickets action card + navBadges.escalations + ticket Show escalation banner + acknowledge endpoint (role:landlord); reassign also clears the open escalation. CARETAKER-PERF: CaretakerPerformanceService (mirror VendorPerformanceService keyed by assigned_to: within-SLA%/avg-resolution/avg-first-response/open/overdue/water-recorded/escalations-raised, batched, landlord-scoped) + MaintenanceCaretakerPerformanceController + Maintenance/CaretakerPerformance.vue + maintenance.caretaker-performance route (role:landlord, linked from the Maintenance hub) + caretaker:performance-rollup weekly gauge (visibility-only). CI: Phase80CaretakerWorkflowSurfaceTest + docs/runbooks/caretaker.md.
closeout.tests: Phase-80 caretaker tests: Escalation 10 + TaskBoard 6 + CaretakerPerf 4 + Surface 8. Pint clean, build clean, nav-audit clean.
closeout.constraints_preserved: Escalate is assignee-only (EscalateTicketRequest), not any caretaker of the landlord; acknowledge/perf are role:landlord; escalate is idempotent on an open escalation (lockForUpdate); auto-escalation is opt-in and resolution-only and caretaker-assigned-only; CaretakerPerformanceService is landlord-scoped + batched (no N+1) with selectRaw static aggregates; first_response_at stamping is reused (TicketActivity non-tenant hook).
closeout.coderabbit: CodeRabbit CLI unavailable in this environment + the agent corrupts the test DB (known gotcha) — manual self-review applied instead: row-locked idempotent escalate; assignee-only request guard; forward-only transition order; landlord-scoped batched performance queries; escalation cleared on both acknowledge and reassign; auto-escalation gated + idempotent; lang parity en/sw/ar.
